description: Returning exit code 0 even though the command failed, making broken steps look successful to shells and CI.
body: Failure mode: an error path prints a message but the process still exits 0 (or never sets a non-zero code), so `set -e`, `&&` chains, and CI all treat the run as success. Detection signals: CI stays green despite visible errors; a failed step does not stop a `&&` pipeline; `echo $?` is 0 after an error. Do not exit 0 on failure. Safer fallback: set a distinct non-zero code on every failure path before exiting, document the codes, and add tests asserting `$?` for success and each failure case.
